title: Kampala's New Flyover Finally Opens to Traffic
author: Musah Kironde
excerpt: After years of construction and diversions, the Clock Tower flyover has opened, cutting travel time across the city centre.
image_alt: Traffic flowing under the newly opened Kampala flyover at dusk
content: Motorists in Kampala woke up to shorter commutes on Tuesday as the long-awaited Clock Tower flyover officially opened to traffic, three years after construction began.
The Uganda National Roads Authority says the interchange is expected to ease congestion at one of the city's busiest junctions, where commuters have endured detours and gridlock since 2023.
"This is a project that has tested the patience of this city, but the outcome speaks for itself," said a UNRA spokesperson at the opening ceremony.
Business owners along the affected route say they are hopeful the improved flow of traffic will bring back customers who avoided the area during construction.
King Musah Media will continue to follow how the new interchange affects commute times across the city in the coming weeks.

title: Parliament Opens Debate on New Digital Services Tax Bill
author: Grace Namutebi
excerpt: Lawmakers are split over a proposed levy on digital platforms, with critics warning it could raise costs for everyday internet users.
image_alt: Members of the Uganda Parliament seated in the chamber during a session
content: The Ugandan Parliament began debate this week on a bill that would introduce a levy on revenue generated by digital service providers operating in the country, including streaming and e-commerce platforms.
Supporters of the bill argue it will widen the tax base at a time when digital commerce is growing quickly, while opponents say the cost is likely to be passed on to consumers.
The Ministry of Finance says the bill is part of a broader push to modernise Uganda's tax system in line with regional partners who have introduced similar measures.
Civil society groups have asked for public hearings before the bill proceeds to a second reading, saying the current draft was tabled with limited consultation.
The committee is expected to report back to the House within three weeks.

title: Uganda Cranes Qualify for AFCON 2027 After Dramatic Win
author: Ivan Ssebunya
excerpt: A last-minute goal sent fans into celebration as the Cranes secured their spot in next year's Africa Cup of Nations.
image_alt: Uganda Cranes players celebrating on the pitch after scoring
content: The Uganda Cranes booked their ticket to AFCON 2027 on Sunday night with a 2-1 win in a qualifier that went down to the final minutes.
Substitute striker Joseph Mutumba scored the winning goal in the 89th minute, sparking wild celebrations among fans who had packed the stadium despite a nervy first half.
Head coach praised the squad's resilience, saying the team "never stopped believing" even after conceding an early equaliser.
This will be Uganda's second consecutive AFCON appearance, and fans are already looking ahead to the group stage draw expected later this year.
Local sports bars across Kampala reported a surge in customers watching the match, with celebrations continuing well into the night.

title: Kampala Music Week Announces Star-Studded Lineup
author: Diana Achieng
excerpt: Organisers have revealed this year's headline acts, promising the biggest edition of the festival yet.
image_alt: A concert crowd with stage lights during a past Kampala Music Week event
content: Kampala Music Week returns next month with its most ambitious lineup to date, organisers announced on Thursday, featuring a mix of established Ugandan artists and emerging talent from across East Africa.
The three-day festival will take place at Lugogo, with organisers promising expanded food and craft vendor areas alongside the main stage.
"We wanted this edition to feel like a true celebration of homegrown talent," said the festival's creative director during the lineup announcement.
Early bird tickets go on sale this weekend, with organisers expecting them to sell out quickly given the demand from previous years.

title: Uganda's Coffee Exporters Report Record Quarterly Earnings
author: Musah Kironde
excerpt: Rising global prices and higher export volumes have pushed coffee earnings to a new quarterly high.
image_alt: Coffee beans drying in the sun at a farm in Uganda
content: Uganda's coffee sector posted its strongest quarterly earnings on record, driven by a combination of higher global prices and increased export volumes, according to figures released this week.
The Uganda Coffee Development Authority says exporters shipped significantly more bags compared to the same period last year, with Robusta varieties leading the growth.
Farmers in the central and western regions say the improved prices have started to translate into better incomes, though some warn that rising input costs are eating into the gains.
Analysts caution that global price swings mean the sector's fortunes could shift quickly, and are calling for continued investment in local value addition.

title: Abasomesa Bakubye Obulumbe ku Nsimbi z'Omushaara
author: Grace Namutebi
excerpt: Abasomesa mu Uganda basabye Gavumenti okwongera ku mushaara nga bwe bagamba nti tegumala.
image_alt: Abasomesa nga bakuŋŋaanidde okulaga obulumbe bwabwe
content: Abasomesa mu Uganda bakubye obulumbe nga basaba Gavumenti okwongera ku mishaara gyabwe, nga bagamba nti egyo ge balina kaakati tegisobola kubamalira ebyetaago by'obulamu.
Omukulembeze w'ekibiina ky'abasomesa yagambye nti bakola nnyo naye ne batasasulwa nga bwe kigwanira, era nti ekyo kikosa n'obulungi bw'okuyigiriza.
Gavumenti egamba nti erina enteekateeka y'okulaba engeri gy'eyinza okuyamba abasomesa, naye nga tewali kiseera kya ddala kye baalangiridde.
Abazadde abamu bagamba nti bali mu kutya nti obulumbe buno buyinza okutabangula essomero ku basomesa singa tewali muddo gutuuka mangu.
